context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=28; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon, or other fruit pieces AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, social with a person on the right, social with a person in front, or watching TV on the right THEN no microwave BECAUSE repeated fruit meals in these contexts were consistently served cold. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as bite-sized sandwiches, bite-sized pizza and broccoli, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, or working on a laptop on the right THEN 2 min or 3 min, with 2 min the stronger overall baseline and 3 min an occasional hotter choice, BECAUSE repeated savory meals across personal, social, TV, and task contexts most often use moderate reheating. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the left THEN 1 min or 2 min BECAUSE left-social savory evidence includes both lighter and moderate reheating choices. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or mixed dessert/fruit meals with sauce such as bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop on the left THEN 2 min or 3 min, with evidence split between moderate and stronger reheating, BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals no longer support only the hottest option. IF food_condition is savory food with sauce or dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front THEN 1 min or 3 min BECAUSE the user has selected both lighter and stronger reheating in that specific laptop-front sauced-food context. IF food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the left THEN 1 min or 2 min BECAUSE left-TV savory evidence supports lighter-to-moderate reheating rather than a single fixed level.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without sauce or dip such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed quiet personal meal did not require preserving a partner or display view. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type including chicken with hash brown and fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-social meals consistently preserve the view toward a front-facing social partner. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-side social meals consistently preserve the view toward a right-side social partner. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or savory food with ketchup such as chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-social meals show a stable partner-oriented preference to keep the left side visible. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or fruit THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the front view while working across different foods. IF dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front-view preservation while attending to a front display. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, or fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals across both savory and fruit foods consistently preserve the view toward the TV on the right. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show a stable preference to keep the left-side display visible. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or warm savory food such as chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE both fruit and savory right-laptop meals show a stable preference to keep the right-side work display visible. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop work meals show that left-side work does not automatically trigger an occlusion-avoidance preference. Across observed contexts, the user tends to protect the view toward attended people or displays except in left-laptop work and quiet personal meals, which favor no occlusion constraint.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without sauce or dip such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN medium BECAUSE the observed quiet personal meal used a moderate pace. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken with hash brown or fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN slow or medium, with evidence now leaning toward slow, BECAUSE one front-social meal used medium while a later front-social fruit meal used slow. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN slow BECAUSE repeated right-side social meals consistently support a cautious pace. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or savory food with ketchup such as chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN slow or medium, with evidence leaning toward slow, BECAUSE one left-social meal used medium while a later left-social meal used slow. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, or fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN medium or slow, with medium better supported overall, BECAUSE earlier and latest right-TV meals used medium while one right-TV savory meal used slow. IF dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN medium BECAUSE the user accepted a moderate pace while attending to a front display. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges THEN medium or fast, with medium more frequently reinforced than fast, BECAUSE the user has selected both but repeatedly accepted medium in laptop-front savory-with-dip meals. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or warm savory food such as chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals across different foods support a stable moderate pace in that work context. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN medium BECAUSE left-laptop meals across multiple foods support a moderate baseline. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN slow or fast BECAUSE one left-TV meal used fast while a later left-TV meal used slow, showing substantial within-context variability.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, bite-sized pizza, chicken strips, chicken breast strips, chicken nuggets, strawberries, bananas, cantaloupes, watermelon, brownies, buffalo chicken bites in work contexts, or other fruit pieces AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option, which is compatible with limited mouth opening, across many foods and contexts, and the new right-TV fruit meal further reinforces this baseline. IF food_condition is savory mixed pieces such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the left THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-acquisition-success option for that meal, indicating a food-specific exception despite the general narrow-bite preference. IF food_condition is savory bite-sized foods with sauce or dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the left or on the right THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated social meals with dippable savory bite items show the user can prioritize acquisition success over narrower bites in those contexts. Across observed tie-break cases, limited mouth opening supports parallel as the broad default, but social meals with dippable savory bites can shift toward perpendicular for acquisition reliability.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, bite-sized pizza and broccoli, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, fruit, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred explicit confirmation of bite acquisition rather than skipping verification across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF user has limited trunk control and cannot lean forward AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type so far THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is poorly matched to the user's physical abilities and the user has consistently selected inside-mouth transfer. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward, making inside-mouth transfer the physically compatible mode. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without sauce or dip such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed quiet personal meal used a multimodal readiness cue. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE one right-TV savory meal showed a context-specific preference for a multimodal readiness cue. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN speech + LED BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal showed a multimodal cue preference there. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals in those foods selected LED-only signaling. IF dining context is watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, fruit, mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN LED, with left-laptop work mixed but leaning LED overall, BECAUSE LED-only signaling has been selected across most previously observed non-personal contexts. Across observed contexts, LED is the broad baseline, but multimodal speech + LED is supported in a quiet personal meal and a few specific task or TV contexts.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is personal, social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type including mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids unreliable open-mouth signaling, and repeated social and personal meals reinforce the same explicit-control pattern. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, fruit, chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method in attention-demanding or display-oriented contexts. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN button or autocontinue, with button better supported as the broader left-TV pattern, BECAUSE the user previously selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation in that context, and later evidence reinforced button.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is personal, social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type including mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids mouth-based readiness detection that may be unreliable for this user, and repeated social and personal meals support the same pattern. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, fruit, chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN button or autocontinue, with button better supported as the broader left-TV pattern, BECAUSE the user previously selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation in that context, and later evidence reinforced button.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is personal, social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type including mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and more reliable than mouth-based signaling, and repeated social and personal meals support the same pattern. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, fruit, chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable readiness method. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN button or autocontinue, with button better supported as the broader left-TV pattern, BECAUSE the user previously selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation in that context, and later evidence reinforced button.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without sauce or dip such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed quiet personal meal used a multimodal completion cue. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE one right-TV savory meal showed a context-specific preference for a multimodal completion cue. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN speech + LED BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal showed a multimodal completion cue preference there. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals in those foods selected LED-only completion signaling. IF dining context is watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, fruit, mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN LED, with left-laptop work mixed but leaning LED overall, BECAUSE LED-only completion signaling has been selected across most previously observed non-personal contexts. Across observed contexts, LED is the broad baseline, but multimodal speech + LED is supported in a quiet personal meal and a few specific task or TV contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food type observed so far AND dining context is personal, social, TV, or work-related THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently accepted the reliable low-effort completion method for bites across all observed contexts, including the new right-TV fruit meal. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND food_condition is any observed food type in those contexts including mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN button BECAUSE perception for drinking depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user, and repeated evidence supports explicit manual completion across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond the stable avoidance of gesture-based perception.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND food_condition is any observed food type in those contexts including mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN button BECAUSE perception for wiping depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user, and repeated evidence supports explicit manual completion across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond the stable avoidance of gesture-based perception.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without sauce or dip such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN no BECAUSE the observed quiet personal meal favored efficiency over extra retraction. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type including chicken with hash brown and mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-facing social meals show a stable preference for retracting to reduce prolonged visual obstruction during interaction. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-side social meals across multiple foods show a stable preference to reduce obstruction during social interaction. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or savory food with ketchup such as chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-social evidence supports the same social-context preference to reduce obstruction for a left-side partner. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges or fruit THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting while engaged with a front-facing task, consistent with wanting to keep the workspace visible. IF dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN yes BECAUSE the user preferred retracting when preserving a front display view during entertainment. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, or fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon THEN no BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals show the user prefers not to add extra meal time in that entertainment context. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show the user prefers not to add extra meal time in that entertainment context as well. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or warm savory food such as chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN no BECAUSE repeated right-side work meals show the user favors efficiency over retracting even while still wanting to minimize right-side occlusion. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-side work meals favor efficiency over retracting. Across observed tie-break cases, social interaction and front-focused visibility concerns outweigh efficiency, while personal, side-TV, and side-work contexts more often favor skipping retraction.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted some dipping while limiting messiness in social contexts. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch or chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN less or do not dip, with less better supported as the broader left-social pattern and do not dip remaining a stricter meal-specific exception, BECAUSE one left-social meal used no dipping while a later left-social savory meal used limited dipping. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred limited dipping, consistent with reducing messiness during an attention-demanding front-focused activity. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals now show the same limited-dipping preference across both dessert and savory sauced foods. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred limited dipping in a front-attention entertainment context. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the right-TV savory meal extends the user's limited-dipping, mess-averse pattern to that entertainment context. IF food has no dip or sauce available AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, watching TV on the left, watching TV on the right, social settings including social with a person in front, and working on a laptop in front, on the right, or on the left THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals across all such contexts, including the new right-TV fruit meal. Across observed sauced-food cases, the user prefers limited rather than heavy dipping, especially in social or work contexts where messiness matters.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without sauce or dip such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed quiet personal meal used a moderate wait rather than the shortest or longest option. IF dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, fruit, mixed fruit such as cantaloupes, bananas, and watermelon, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the longest wait in these non-personal contexts, reinforcing a strong preference to avoid automatic progression without ample time to intervene, and the new right-TV fruit meal further strengthens that pattern. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or bite-sized sandwiches THEN 10 sec or 1000 sec BECAUSE one left-TV meal used a very short wait while a later left-TV meal used the longest wait, showing substantial within-context variability. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally prefers long waits when maintaining explicit control in social, work, and most TV contexts, while the quiet personal meal supports a shorter moderate wait.
